_meta.note: Built to the Armagh-locked standard, though London is a lighter record by nature: an overseas team in the Connacht championship with a four-game season (one Connacht game, three Tailteann Cup games). Scorer detail is sparse in the available coverage for London's games and is recorded honestly where found.
_meta.verification: All scorelines and venues verified against the official RTE and Connacht GAA results tables and primary match reports (gaa.ie, the42, Irish Times, Laois Today). Note one cross-source scoreline discrepancy on the Sligo Round 3 game, flagged in that game's note.

manager.note: Manager name not sourced in the available 2026 coverage. Left null rather than assumed.

league.note: London competed in Division 4 of the National League. Exact final position and round-by-round results not sourced.

scorers.note: goal a minute into the second half, the game's only goal

source.snippet: London Quarter-Final 1-15 0-31 Mayo Venue: McGovern Park, Ruislip Referee: Paddy Neilan

results.note: Connacht opener at home to Mayo. London actually opened the scoring through Joe McGill and traded early scores before Mayo's two-pointers pulled them clear to an 11-point half-time lead. The 0-31 Mayo total was the most points by one team in the 2026 Connacht series. Full London scorer list not sourced. Referee: Paddy Neilan.

source.snippet: London fired five goals past Waterford as they stormed to a 17-point win in their Tailteann Cup opener in Dungarvan.

results.note: A landmark day for London: five goals (four in the first half) and a 17-point win, their first-round Tailteann victory. Waterford goalkeeper Paudie Hunt fumbled an early McGill shot for the first goal, with Rafter, Davis and Gouldson adding the others before half-time and Crowley a fifth after the break. Goal scorers identified; full point breakdown not sourced.

scorers.note: goal that briefly cut the gap to three

source.snippet: Tailteann Cup Round 2A Laois 1-29 London 1-14... London came with a surge. Sub Tighe Barry started a move that was finished to the net by Michael O'Reilly.

results.note: Led briefly into the second half (1-14 to 1-11 at one stage) before Laois scored nine in a row to win by 15, a record-high Laois championship tally. London's goal came from Michael O'Reilly, set up by sub Tighe Barry. This Round 2A loss dropped London into the Round 3 knockout. Full London scorer list not sourced.

source.snippet: Round 3 6 Jun London 0-16 2-16 Sligo McGovern Park, Ruislip

results.note: Elimination game. London led 0-6 to 0-4 early and were level several times before two second-half Sligo goals (Gorman, Mulligan) settled it. Scoreline discrepancy noted: the RTE and Irish Times results give the final as London 0-16 Sligo 2-16 (used here); one gaa.ie/Examiner match report rendered an in-game figure of Sligo 2-13 to London 0-13. The official results-table total (2-16 to 0-16) is treated as authoritative and the report figure flagged. London's own perspective recorded here; Sligo's file carries the opposite side.

Full scorer lists for the Mayo Connacht QF, the Waterford R1 five-goal win, and the Laois R2A game (goal scorers and standout names identified, full point breakdowns not published in available coverage)
collection_meta.notes: London is a deliberately lighter record, appropriate to an overseas entrant with a four-game season and thin published coverage. The one genuinely notable beat is the five-goal demolition of Waterford in the Tailteann Cup Round 1, a landmark win for the Exiles. Shay Rafter and Joe McGill are the recurring names across their campaign. Scorer detail is fullest for the Sligo elimination game; honest nulls elsewhere rather than invented tallies.
